sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 15

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 15:1
items.html:
(to the question) Ben Bechor [firstborn son]
Just as the upper one is discerned as a father to the lower one, being the cause of its emanation, with the lower one caused by it — so there is one discernment in the upper one from whose perspective it is discerned only as a brother to the lower one. This means that in this respect the lower one is not caused by the upper one; rather, the lower one and the upper one are both together caused by the one above the upper one. And since both are caused together by a single cause, in this they are considered only brothers, sons of one father.
